confirmed.title: AiExtractInput empty-state 'No extraction yet — click button to start.' hardcoded English
confirmed.detail:

. Every other user-facing string in this same file is wrapped in t() (the action buttons at line 268, the config error at 189, the status aria-label 'Extracting' at 271). This empty-state — arguably the most-seen state before any extraction runs — bypasses the translator entirely. NOT in the i18n-fixed-ledger (which covered lines 189 and 268 in this file but not 331). confirmed.title: CommandPalette 'No commands found' empty-results message hardcoded English
confirmed.detail: Visible JSX text node inside the results <li role="presentation"> rendered when groups.length === 0. The literal `No commands found` is not wrapped in t(), even though this component is one of the most heavily-localized in the codebase: the surrounding sr-only title (274), input placeholder (285), live-region count (291), listbox aria-label (297/320), and footer hints (356-358) are ALL keyed via t(). The no-results state is a primary, frequently-seen palette state. NOT in the ledger (which covers 274/285/291/297/356-358 but skips 325). Visible text, not aria.
confirmed.why_real: Verified at /home/diogo/PhpstormProjects/arqel/packages-js/ui/src/palette/CommandPalette.tsx:325. The empty-results state renders a hardcoded English JSX text node `No commands found` inside `<li role="presentation">` (rendered when `groups.length === 0`), not wrapped in t(). Every other string in this same component is localized via t(): placeholder (306), aria live-region (312-314), listbox aria-label (320), footer hints (per ledger 356-358). This is a genuine, user-facing missing-i18n defect (visible text, not aria) in a primary/frequently-seen palette state, and the inconsistency with the rest of the file confirms it's an oversight. The file lives in src/palette/, not src/shadcn/*, so it is not a vendored primitive. Checked the ledger (reports/i18n-fixed-ledger.json): CommandPalette.tsx entries cover lines 274, 285, 291, 297 (round 1), 356/357/358 (round 8), and 111/154 (round 9) — line 325 is NOT present. Novel, reproduces, not vendored, not previously fixed in R1-R20.

confirmed.title: InertiaInspector tab label 'Page Props' hardcoded English
confirmed.detail: Visible <button role="tab"> face text `Page Props` is a bare English literal. The file imports `t` from './i18n.js' and uses it for the filter placeholder (devtools.filter_placeholder) and other controls, but the three tab labels are unlocalized and have no corresponding keys in src/panel/i18n.ts. The ledger lists InertiaInspector only for inertia.filter_aria / inertia.copy_aria — the visible tab labels were missed. Visible text, not aria.
confirmed.why_real:
Confirmed real and novel. At /home/diogo/PhpstormProjects/arqel/packages-js/devtools-extension/src/panel/InertiaInspector.tsx the three visible sub-tab button labels are bare English literals: line 89 `Page Props`, line 98 `Shared Props`, line 107 `Navigation History` (plus lines 125 `Copied!`/`Copy` and 139 empty-state, all hardcoded too). The file imports `t` from './i18n.js' (line 14) and uses it for the filter placeholder (devtools.filter_placeholder) and aria-labels (devtools.inertia.filter_aria, devtools.inertia.copy_aria), proving localization intent; the tab labels are inconsistent omissions. No corresponding keys (page_props/shared_props/navigation_history) exist in src/panel/i18n.ts.

Not a duplicate. The ledger has only two InertiaInspector entries: round 10 line 145 (navigation timestamp toLocaleTimeString, a different defect) and round 19 line 112 ("filter placeholder + aria-labels hardcoded" — exactly the controls the finding acknowledges were covered, NOT the tab labels). The round-19 "DevTools top-level tab labels hardcoded" entry (line 82) is in App.tsx — those are the top-level panel tabs (Inertia/Policy/Fields), a different component from the Page Props/Shared Props/Navigation History sub-tabs inside InertiaInspector. So the visible sub-tab labels were genuinely missed.

Not vendored: file lives in src/panel/, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*. User-facing visible text (button face text, not aria).

confirmed.title: InertiaInspector tab label 'Shared Props' hardcoded English
confirmed.detail: Visible <button role="tab"> face text `Shared Props` is a bare English literal, unwrapped, with no key in src/panel/i18n.ts, despite the file otherwise using t(). Same gap as the 'Page Props' tab. Visible text, not aria.
confirmed.why_real:
Verified at /home/diogo/PhpstormProjects/arqel/packages-js/devtools-extension/src/panel/InertiaInspector.tsx:98. The visible <button role="tab"> face text `Shared Props` is a bare, unwrapped English literal (siblings `Page Props` at L89 and `Navigation History` at L107 share the same gap). The file otherwise uses t() at L113 (devtools.filter_placeholder), L117, L123, so the inconsistency is genuine. i18n.ts has NO devtools.inertia.tab.* keys, so non-English (pt_BR via navigator.language) users see English tab labels — real, user-facing.

Not vendored: lives in packages-js/devtools-extension/src/panel/, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*.

Not a duplicate of R1-R20: the ledger's only InertiaInspector hit in that window is round 19 at line 112, titled 'InertiaInspector filter placeholder + aria-labels hardcoded' — that fix (now present at L113/117/123) explicitly scopes to the filter input placeholder and the filter/copy aria-labels, NOT the visible tab face text. The round-22 entry (L1373) is unrelated (navigation timestamp toLocaleTimeString locale). The App.tsx round-19 entry (L1763) covers the *top-level* DevTools tab labels in App.tsx, a different file/widget. So the visible InertiaInspector sub-tab labels were never addressed in R1-R20.

confirmed.title: InertiaInspector tab label 'Navigation History' hardcoded English
confirmed.detail: Visible <button role="tab"> face text `Navigation History` is a bare English literal, unwrapped, with no key in src/panel/i18n.ts, despite the file otherwise using t(). Completes the trio of unlocalized InertiaInspector tab labels. Visible text, not aria.
confirmed.why_real:
CONFIRMED. Verified at packages-js/devtools-extension/src/panel/InertiaInspector.tsx:107: the `<button type="button" role="tab" ... data-testid="tab-navigation">` renders the bare English literal `Navigation History` with no t() wrapping, while the file imports `{ t }` from './i18n.js' (line 14) and routes its controls through it (filter placeholder line 113, aria-labels lines 117/123). The sibling tab labels Page Props (line 89) and Shared Props (line 98) are likewise hardcoded — the trio of inspector sub-tab labels is entirely unlocalized.

NOVEL / NOT A DUPLICATE: The i18n-fixed-ledger.json has exactly two InertiaInspector.tsx entries: (1) round 10, line 145 — the navigation-event TIMESTAMP toLocaleTimeString() no-locale-arg; (2) round 19, line 112 — explicitly scoped to "filter placeholder + aria-labels" (the controls block, lines 112-123). The round-19 detection note confirms its scope is "line 112 placeholder, line 116/122 aria-labels" only. Neither entry touches the visible tab-button labels. The round-19 "top-level tab labels hardcoded" entry is for the DIFFERENT file App.tsx (line 82). The bundled i18n.ts dictionary has devtools.tab.* keys only for the App-level panel tabs (inertia/policies/fields/time_travel/performance) and no key for the inspector's internal Page Props / Shared Props / Navigation History sub-tabs — confirming there is no localization path. So this string is unledgered across R1-R20.

NOT VENDORED: file lives in packages-js/devtools-extension/src/panel/, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*. First-party authored component.

USER-FACING: i18n.ts activeLocale() derives from navigator.language; with pt-BR the surrounding devtools chrome renders translated (e.g. devtools.tab.inertia => 'Inspetor de Estado Inertia') while these tab faces stay English. Project precedent (rounds 9/10/12/19/20) consistently admits devtools-extension panel strings as in-scope i18n defects.

confirmed.title: TrashedFilter top-level filter label hardcoded to English 'Trashed' (framework default, never a translation key)
confirmed.detail: TrashedFilter::make() sets the filter's own visible name via $filter->label('Trashed'). At serialization Filter::getLabel() routes the value through localizeLabel(), which calls trans('Trashed'); because 'Trashed' is a literal display string and not a registered translation key, trans() returns it verbatim, so the filter's chrome label always renders English regardless of the active locale (pt_BR etc.). This is distinct from the soft-delete OPTION labels at line 134-136 ('Without deleted'/'With deleted'/'Only deleted'), which were already fixed in ledger round 8 (line 134); the top-level filter NAME was never covered by any round. Framework-hardcoded: the default should be a translation key such as 'arqel::messages.filter.trashed' (added to the en + pt_BR message catalogs) so the built-in filter name localizes out of the box, matching how the option-label defaults and other built-in filters are handled. App-can-override remains available via Filter::label().
confirmed.why_real:
Verified in /home/diogo/PhpstormProjects/arqel/packages/table/src/Filters/TrashedFilter.php:55: TrashedFilter::make() sets $filter->label('Trashed'). Filter::getLabel() (packages/table/src/Filter.php:100) routes this through localizeLabel() (line 110), which calls trans('Trashed') (line 116). 'Trashed' is a plain English display literal, not a registered translation key, and it is absent from any arqel/table lang catalog (no package lang dir defines it). Per the documented localizeLabel contract, a literal with no matching translation passes through unchanged, so the filter's chrome NAME renders English under every locale (pt_BR etc.). This is a genuine user-facing i18n defect in PHP framework code, not a vendored shadcn primitive (not in packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*).

Not a duplicate: the i18n-fixed-ledger.json has exactly one TrashedFilter entry (round 8) and it explicitly targets line 134 — the soft-delete OPTION labels 'Without deleted'/'With deleted'/'Only deleted' (now wrapped in localizeLabel at lines 134-136). The top-level filter NAME at line 55 is a distinct string at a distinct location and was never covered by rounds 1-20. The fix is also distinct: convert the make() default to a real key (e.g. arqel::messages.filter.trashed) registered in en + pt_BR catalogs, mirroring how the option-label defaults are handled, while leaving Filter::label() available for app overrides.
